quotes.text: It's quite valuable to have the courage and the confidence to say, 'No, fuck off, leave me alone, thank you very much.'
quotes.speaker: Helen Mirren
quotes.context: In a 2013 Daily Mail interview, the Oscar-winning actress said that if she'd had a daughter, "fuck off" is the first phrase she would have taught her, to give her the confidence to set boundaries.

quotes.text: People meet me and go 'oh, the wholesome dad!' and I have to gently explain that the man on television and the man at the two a.m. show are legally separate individuals. One of them coaches little league. The other one is not allowed within five hundred feet of a brunch.
quotes.speaker: Bob Saget
quotes.context: On his stand-up vs. TV reputation, a 2014 podcast appearance
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the 'wholesome dad on TV, filthy animal on stage' contrast was Saget's whole bit. The tell: the real Bob would've immediately given you a specific, far more graphic example; this one stays coy.

quotes.text: A couple nights ago, I was licking jelly off my boyfriend's penis. And I thought, "Oh my God — I'm turning into my mother!"
quotes.speaker: Sarah Silverman
quotes.context: From her 2005 stand-up special "Sarah Silverman: Jesus Is Magic." Her signature sweet-faced delivery lands a graphic sex image as the setup, with the punchline being the realization she's inheriting her mother's habits.

quotes.text: I went to one of these fancy joints where the portions are the size of a postage stamp and the bill is the size of a phone book. The waiter goes 'and how did we find everything?' I said WE didn't find nothing — I'm still LOOKING. Bring me a real plate before I start in on the centerpiece, because I will eat the flowers, pal. I've done it before.
quotes.speaker: Buddy Hackett
quotes.context: A raunchy 1970s Las Vegas nightclub set
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the borscht-belt restaurant gripe escalating into 'I'll eat the flowers' is exactly Buddy Hackett's nightclub register. The tell: the real Buddy would've detoured into something filthy halfway through; this one stays on the food.

quotes.text: I tried one of those standing desks. You know what a standing desk is for a man my size? It's called STANDING. We had the technology the whole time, we just kept sitting on it. I stood up for one meeting and three coworkers asked if I was okay. I said no, Karen, I am WORKING OUT.
quotes.speaker: Ralphie May
quotes.context: On modern office culture, a 2015 stand-up taping
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the self-deprecating size humor with a sudden specific target ('Karen') is the Ralphie May move. The tell: the real Ralphie would've taken it somewhere far more brutal and personal.

quotes.text: My beauty routine? I get up, I put on my face, and I apologize to it. I told my plastic surgeon to do whatever he wanted — he took one look and billed me for a full demolition. My husband Fang said I looked refreshed. I said Fang, you've been asleep since Tuesday.
quotes.speaker: Phyllis Diller
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the self-roasting beauty jokes and the 'Fang' husband bit are pure Phyllis Diller. The tell: the real Phyllis fired these as rapid one-liners; this one lingers a beat too long on the setup.
